description: Electron / Tauri / WebView2 desktop application security patterns. Mostly static-analysis-driven (binary inspection by desktop-agent); only autoupdate_http_mitm participates in auto_probe via HTTP. Reference contexts emit grep/probe instructions in their `detect` field for operator + desktop-agent.

node_integration_enabled.explanation: Renderer process can require('child_process'). Any XSS in remote-loaded page becomes RCE in main process.

context_isolation_disabled.detect: grep -RE 'contextIsolation\s*:\s*false' extracted/ — also flag missing flag on Electron<12 (default false)
context_isolation_disabled.explanation: Preload world and renderer world share globals. Renderer prototype pollution rewrites preload functions (Discord 2020 chain).

sandbox_disabled.explanation: Without OS-level sandbox, V8 patch-gap bugs reach full host process privileges.

preload_dangerous_exposure.explanation: Preload exposes dangerous primitives directly to the renderer's main world. Renderer XSS reaches native power.

shell_openexternal_unfiltered.detect: grep -R 'shell.openExternal\|shell.openPath' -B2 -A5 extracted/ — flag callers with no scheme allowlist
shell_openexternal_unfiltered.explanation: shell.openExternal hands URL to OS handler. javascript:/file:/data:/UNC/ms-msdt: schemes execute. Rocket.Chat H1 #924151 class.

ipc_handler_no_origin_check.detect: grep -R 'ipcMain.on\|ipcMain.handle' -A 10 extracted/ — flag handlers missing event.sender.getURL() allowlist
ipc_handler_no_origin_check.explanation: ipcMain.handle('exec', (e, cmd) => exec(cmd)) with no origin gate. Renderer XSS → main-process RCE.

custom_protocol_traversal.explanation: Custom URL schemes parse path inputs without traversal filter. file:// fallthrough discloses local files.

electron_fuse_runasnode_enabled.detect: npx @electron/fuses read --app <path> — flag RunAsNode=Enabled OR EnableNodeCliInspectArguments=Enabled
electron_fuse_runasnode_enabled.explanation: RunAsNode fuse turns Electron binary into a Node interpreter for ELECTRON_RUN_AS_NODE. Combined with NodeCliInspect, attacker forks self with --inspect-brk and runs arbitrary JS as the app.

electron_fuse_no_asar_integrity.explanation: Without ASAR integrity fuses, attacker swaps app.asar (or stages malicious resource override) and the app loads it without signature check.

videoframe_contextbridge_bypass.detect: grep preload sources for 'VideoFrame' near 'exposeInMainWorld' — Electron 39<39.8 / 40<40.7 / 41<41.0-beta.8 vulnerable
videoframe_contextbridge_bypass.explanation: Bridging a VideoFrame via contextBridge leaks the isolated world's MainWorldExecutionContext. Main-world XSS reaches isolated world.

v8_patch_gap.detect: DevTools: process.versions.electron / process.versions.chrome — cross-reference with `kev_epss_enrich` against Chrome stable. Patch lag >6 weeks = exploitable.
v8_patch_gap.explanation: Electron lags Chrome stable. Public CVEs in V8 / Blink work against shipped Electron apps. ContextIsolation does NOT protect from memory bugs that bypass isolate.

autoupdate_http_mitm.description: Update manifest fetched over plaintext HTTP — MITM via match_replace to swap binary URL / hash. Squirrel.Mac / electron-updater pattern.
autoupdate_http_mitm.explanation: Captured update-channel HTTP request → match_replace the manifest 'url' field to attacker host → trojan update installs on next launch.

tauri_capability_overbroad.detect: cat src-tauri/capabilities/*.json + tauri.conf.json — flag fs:allow-* without scope, shell:allow-execute without args allowlist, http:default without origin allowlist
tauri_capability_overbroad.explanation: Tauri v2 capability model: per-window permission grants. Missing scope on fs / shell / http capabilities = arbitrary host access from any window.

webview2_host_object_exposed.detect: grep host C#/C++ for CoreWebView2.AddHostObjectToScript — flag if remote URL navigable in WebView
webview2_host_object_exposed.explanation: AddHostObjectToScript exposes .NET / native objects to JS in WebView. Navigation to attacker URL reaches the host bridge.

webview2_postmessage_no_origin.detect: WebMessageReceived handler with no e.Source / e.Origin allowlist
webview2_postmessage_no_origin.explanation: WebView2 host's WebMessageReceived trusts message regardless of source origin. Foreign-origin XSS in WebView reaches host.
